LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELIZABETH KOWAL'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT DEFENDANT ELIZABETH KOWAL PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED HERSELF OF THE FORUM BY PURCHASING PROPERTY IN CALIFORNIA IN 2004. (DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ("RJN"), EX. A.) MRS. KOWAL ALSO IS LISTED ON A DEED OF TRUST FOR THE PROPERTY, WHICH INDICATES THAT SHE HAD CONTRACTED TO MAKE MONTHLY PAYMENTS TO A LENDER LOCATED IN CALIFORNIA FOR A 30 YEAR PERIOD. (RJN, EX. C.) THE EVIDENCE FURTHER ESTABLISHES THAT THE SUIT IS RELATED TO MRS. KOWAL'S ACTIVITIES IN CALIFORNIA, AS THE COMPLAINT SEEKS RECOVERY OF HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION ASSESSMENTS DUE BY VIRTUE OF HER OWNERSHIP OF THE PROPERTY. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED THAT THERE ARE REQUISITE MINIMUM CONTACTS SUPPORTING SPECIFIC PERSONAL JURISDICTION, WHICH SHIFTS THE BURDEN TO DEFENDANT TO SHOW THAT JURISDICTION IS NOT REASONABLE. (INTEGRAL DEVELOPMENT CORP. V. WEISSENBACH (2002) 99 CAL.APP.4TH 576, 591.) DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET HER BURDEN. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons;
Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Nathaniel Dunn, Esq. of Chapman & Intrieri, LLP, Tel. 510.864.3600, for plaintiff and prevailing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested but modified it because of a clerical error. DEFENDANT ELIZABETH KOWAL's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is Denied. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. The evidence establishes that Defendant Elizabeth Kowal purposefully availed herself of the forum by purchasing property in California in 2004. (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice ("RJN"), Ex. A.) Mrs. Kowal also is listed on a Deed of Trust for the property, which indicates that she had contracted to make monthly payments to a lender located in California for a 30 year period. (RJN, Ex. C.) The evidence further establishes that the suit is related to Mrs. Kowal's activities in California, as the complaint seeks recovery of Homeowner's Association assessments due by virtue of her ownership of the property. Therefore, Plaintiff has established that there are requisite minimum contacts supporting specific personal jurisdiction, which shifts the burden to defendant to show that jurisdiction is not reasonable. (Integral Development Corp. v. Weissenbach (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 576, 591.) Defendant has not met her burden. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
